prompt:
Platform-tooling ticket. Our Java settings toolkit (SettingsClient + Json) can already read Dynatrace OpenPipeline log-pipeline objects, and the observability team now wants it to *manage* them: our compliance processors (everything under the acme. id prefix) keep getting hand-edited or dropped in the UI, and last month someone's UI save silently clobbered a colleague's concurrent change. Build the missing PipelineReconciler on top of the existing client. Heads up on the API itself: the ticket was originally written against the dedicated OpenPipeline Configurations endpoints, but I've heard those were being sunset — figure out what the current interface actually is and use that; if the ticket's endpoint really is gone, say so in your summary. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract — run: java TestMain.java.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Dynatrace documentation — the OpenPipeline API reference and its Settings-API migration page on docs.dynatrace.com, the Settings API key-concepts page, and the GET-objects / GET-an-object / PUT-an-object reference pages (the settings schema JSON shipped in Dynatrace's official terraform provider on GitHub also counts as first-party for the pipeline value shape). Blogs and community posts don't count. Nail down: which schema IDs now carry OpenPipeline pipeline configuration and what lives inside a settings object's value for builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines (stages, processors, processor types and their per-type attribute objects, id/matcher constraints), how updateToken optimistic locking works and what you must do in the fields parameter to even receive it, what validateOnly does, the exact PUT body shape, what a 409 means and what the error envelope's constraintViolations carry. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. Compare the live docs against them — if anything disagrees, report the conflict in your summary and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests drive (pinned shapes and merge rules in docs/contract.json):
- Existing behavior stays: SettingsClient.listObjects walks cursor pages correctly and SettingsApiException decodes the error envelope.
- new PipelineReconciler(client, ownedPrefix).reconcile(schemaId, desiredPipelines) where each desired entry is {customId, processors}: matches pipelines by value.customId from a fresh list (requesting the documented fields so the concurrency token is actually present), merges owned processors per the pinned merge rule while preserving unowned processors and every other value field verbatim, dry-run-validates before every real write, writes with the concurrency token (never bypassing the check), refetches-and-retries exactly once on a concurrency conflict re-merging against the fresh value, decodes validation conflicts into the outcome, rejects desired processors outside the owned prefix before any request, and writes nothing for converged or missing pipelines. Outcomes per desired pipeline: updated / unchanged / missing / invalid (with violations) / conflict.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — it runs a loopback fake Settings API; no real tenant, no credentials, no Thread.sleep. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; Json.java, SettingsClient.java and SettingsApiException.java are yours to extend.

research.interface: Settings API 2.0 (/api/v2/settings/objects) with builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines schema v1.68 — the OpenPipeline Configurations API it replaces reached end of life 2026-06-29

official_sources.title: Migrate OpenPipeline configurations to Settings API | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the OpenPipeline Configurations API endpoints (GET/PUT /platform/openpipeline/v1/configurations[/{id}]) can no longer be used; replacement Settings API schemas builtin:openpipeline.<configuration.scope>.{routing,pipelines,ingest-sources} (e.g. builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines); scopes move from openpipeline:configurations:read/write to settings.read/settings.write; a settings object wraps its configuration in the value object

official_sources.title: OpenPipeline API | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the deprecation notice: 'The Configurations API is deprecated and will reach its end of life on June 29, 2026', and the pointer to the Settings API migration

official_sources.title: Settings API key concepts | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: updateToken optimistic-locking semantics (write proceeds only if the object is unchanged; omitting the token bypasses the check); fields replaces the default field set entirely and updateToken must be requested explicitly; validateOnly=true runs full server-side validation without persisting; cursor pagination where nextPageKey must be the only parameter on subsequent pages

official_sources.title: Settings API - GET objects | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/v2/settings/objects with schemaIds/fields/pageSize (default 100, max 500); supported fields list incl. objectId, value, scope, schemaVersion, updateToken; response {items, totalCount, pageSize, nextPageKey}; settings.read scope

official_sources.title: Settings API - GET an object | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/v2/settings/objects/{objectId} returns the full object including value, updateToken and schemaVersion — used for the post-409 refetch

official_sources.title: Settings API - PUT an object | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: PUT /api/v2/settings/objects/{objectId} with body {value, updateToken, schemaVersion}; validateOnly query parameter; response codes 200/400/404/409 with SettingsObjectResponse {code, objectId, error{code,message,constraintViolations[{message,parameterLocation,path}]}, invalidValue}; settings.write scope

official_sources.title: builtin:openpipeline.logs.pipelines settings schema v1.68 (official Dynatrace terraform provider source)
official_sources.used_for: the pipeline value shape: customId/displayName/stage fields (processing, davis, metricExtraction, storage, ...), Stage {processors[]} with unique ids, Processor common fields (id, type, enabled, matcher, description, sampleData) and per-type attribute objects (dql{script}, fieldsAdd{fields[{name,value}]}, fieldsRemove{fields}), ProcessorType enum, the id pattern forbidding dt./dynatrace. prefixes, matcher NOT_BLANK/4096, and the routing enum routable/notRoutable

research.language_rationale: The pipeline-config reconciler extends our existing Java settings toolkit (SettingsClient). Dynatrace publishes no supported Java SDK for the Settings API; the documented integration surface is plain REST/JSON, which java.net.http.HttpClient covers idiomatically with zero dependencies.
